openmeter/ledger/chargeadapter.purpose: Bridges charge lifecycle events (credit purchase, flat fee, usage-based) to double-entry ledger postings by translating domain charge state transitions into typed transaction templates committed via ledger.Ledger.CommitGroup. Each charge type has a private handler implementing the corresponding charge-domain Handler interface, keeping charge packages free of ledger imports.

patterns.name: Handler-per-charge-type with compile-time assertion
patterns.description: Each charge type (creditPurchase, flatFee, usageBased) has a private handler struct and a public New*Handler constructor returning the charge-domain Handler interface, guarded by a var _ <interface> = (*handler)(nil) assertion.
patterns.example: var _ chargecreditpurchase.Handler = (*creditPurchaseHandler)(nil)

patterns.name: Resolve-then-annotate-then-commit pipeline
patterns.description: Every ledger write follows transactions.ResolveTransactions then per-input transactions.WithAnnotations then h.ledger.CommitGroup(ctx, transactions.GroupInputs(...)). Never call CommitGroup with un-resolved/un-annotated inputs.
patterns.example: inputs, _ := transactions.ResolveTransactions(ctx, h.deps, scope, template); for i := range inputs { inputs[i] = transactions.WithAnnotations(inputs[i], annotations) }

patterns.name: ChargeAnnotations on every CommitGroup
patterns.description: Every group gets charge-scoped annotations via chargeAnnotationsForXxxCharge -> chargeTransactionAnnotations -> ledger.ChargeTransactionAnnotations (chargeID, namespace, subscription/phase/item IDs, featureID).
patterns.example: annotations := chargeAnnotationsForFlatFeeCharge(charge)

patterns.name: Settlement mode guard before any ledger work
patterns.description: Invoice-side accrual methods call validateSettlementMode with an explicit allowlist of permitted modes; incompatible modes return an error, not a no-op.
patterns.example: if err := validateSettlementMode(input.Charge.Intent.SettlementMode, productcatalog.InvoiceOnlySettlementMode); err != nil { return ..., err }

patterns.name: Return empty GroupReference on zero-amount
patterns.description: Handler methods return an empty ledgertransaction.GroupReference{} (not an error, not a zero-value ledger group) when the amount is zero.
patterns.example: if amount.IsZero() { return ledgertransaction.GroupReference{}, nil }

patterns.name: Delegate FBO collection to collector.Service
patterns.description: flatFeeHandler and usageBasedHandler delegate FBO->accrued collection to collector.Service.CollectToAccrued / CorrectCollectedAccrued; only payment authorization/settlement call ledger directly.
patterns.example: realizations, err := h.collector.CollectToAccrued(ctx, collector.CollectToAccruedInput{...})

patterns.name: clock.Now() for payment timestamps
patterns.description: Payment-authorized and payment-settled methods book at clock.Now() (wall-clock event time); Intent.InvoiceAt is used only for accrual booking. Tests freeze clock to assert this.
patterns.example: transactions.AuthorizeCustomerReceivablePaymentTemplate{ At: clock.Now(), ... }

key_file_guides.role: Constructs models.Annotations for each charge type via ledger.ChargeTransactionAnnotations; imported by all three handler files.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a field to charge.Intent.Subscription (meta.SubscriptionReference) requires threading it through chargeTransactionAnnotations or it is silently omitted from all ledger annotations.

key_file_guides.role: Handles promotional, external, and invoice-settled credit purchase lifecycles; issueCreditPurchase combines advance-attribution, accrued-translation, and receivable-issuance in one CommitGroup.
key_file_guides.watch_for: outstandingAdvanceBalance / unattributedAccruedBalance reads happen before ResolveTransactions and are outside the ledger transaction — ordering matters for correctness.

key_file_guides.role: Flat-fee invoice-assignment, invoice/credits-only usage accrual, payment authorization and settlement. invoiceCostBasis constant (1.0) for invoice-backed transactions.
key_file_guides.watch_for: OnAssignedToInvoice / OnCreditsOnlyUsageAccrued return creditrealization.CreateAllocationInputs (via collector); OnInvoiceUsageAccrued and payment handlers return ledgertransaction.GroupReference — different return types.
Authorization/settlement timestamps use clock.Now(), not charge.Intent.InvoiceAt; tests freeze clock to assert this.

key_file_guides.role: settledBalanceForSubAccount wraps SubAccount.GetBalance returning only Settled().
key_file_guides.watch_for: Returns settled portion only; do not call Pending() here.

anti_patterns: Calling h.ledger.CommitGroup without first resolving through transactions.ResolveTransactions (bypasses sub-account routing)
Omitting chargeAnnotations from a CommitGroup call (breaks per-charge traceability)
Skipping validateSettlementMode in any new lifecycle event handler method
Using charge.Intent.InvoiceAt as the booking timestamp in payment-authorized/settled events (must use clock.Now())
Writing tests by mocking the ledger instead of using ledgertestutils.IntegrationEnv (loses sub-account routing coverage)

decisions.decision: Handler interfaces are defined in the charge sub-packages (chargecreditpurchase.Handler etc.), not in chargeadapter.
decisions.rationale: Keeps charge domain packages free of ledger imports; chargeadapter is the bridge that knows both sides without creating circular imports.

decisions.decision: invoiceCostBasis is a package-level constant (=1) used for all invoice-backed accrual and payment transactions.
decisions.rationale: Invoice-backed receivables have a known 1:1 cost basis; encoding it once prevents per-call divergence.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new flat-fee lifecycle event handler

openmeter/ledger/collector.purpose: Implements FBO-to-accrued credit collection and lineage-aware correction (reversal) as a standalone service consumed by chargeadapter's flat-fee and usage-based handlers. Encapsulates advance shortfall issuance for CreditOnly mode and plan-then-execute merging for multi-step accrual corrections.

patterns.name: Service interface with two operations only
patterns.description: collector.Service exposes exactly CollectToAccrued and CorrectCollectedAccrued; implementation delegates to private accrualCollector (collect.go) and accrualCorrector (correct.go) built in NewService(Config).
patterns.example: func NewService(config Config) Service { return &service{collector: &accrualCollector{...}, corrector: &accrualCorrector{...}} }

patterns.name: Advance shortfall issuance in collect (CreditOnly only)
patterns.description: After resolving FBO->accrued templates, collect appends IssueCustomerReceivable + TransferCustomerFBOAdvanceToAccrued for any shortfall only when settlement mode is CreditOnly. CreditThenInvoice never issues advance.
patterns.example: if shortfall := input.Amount.Sub(collectedFBOAmount); c.shouldAdvanceShortfall(input, shortfall) { inputs = append(inputs, advanceInputs...) }

patterns.name: Credit realization rows from FBO debits
patterns.description: toCreditRealizations emits one creditrealization.CreateAllocationInput per resolved entry that debits AccountTypeCustomerFBO, sharing the group's transactionGroupID.
patterns.example: if entry.Amount().IsNegative() && entry.PostingAddress().AccountType() == ledger.AccountTypeCustomerFBO { out = append(out, creditrealization.CreateAllocationInput{Amount: entry.Amount().Abs()}) }

patterns.name: Plan-then-execute with per-transaction merging
patterns.description: accrualCorrector.correct builds []plannedAction, merges overlapping corrections by transaction ID into one aggregated amount per original transaction, then CorrectTransaction per ID in correctionOrder. Reordering breaks the merge invariant.
patterns.example: mergedByTx := mergeByTransactionID(actions); for _, txID := range correctionOrder { c.ledger.CommitGroup(ctx, mergedByTx[txID]) }

patterns.name: Lineage-aware segment dispatch in planCorrection
patterns.description: When LineageSegmentsByRealization is present, planCorrection dispatches per segment.State: RealCredit -> source correction, AdvanceUncovered -> advance correction, AdvanceBackfilled -> re-issue to FBO.
patterns.example: switch segment.State { case LineageSegmentStateRealCredit: return plannedSourceCorrectionActions(source, amount, false) }

key_file_guides.role: Public Service interface, Config, CollectToAccruedInput, CorrectCollectedAccruedInput, NewService. Entry point for chargeadapter handlers.
key_file_guides.watch_for: CollectToAccruedInput.Annotations is optional (synthesized from Namespace+ChargeID if nil); CorrectCollectedAccruedInput.LineageSegmentsByRealization may be empty for legacy data and must be handled gracefully.

key_file_guides.role: accrualCollector — FBO->accrued collection and advance shortfall issuance; returns one CreateAllocationInput per FBO debit.
key_file_guides.watch_for: shouldAdvanceShortfall returns true only for CreditOnlySettlementMode; never issue advances for CreditThenInvoice.

key_file_guides.role: accrualCorrector — lineage-aware reversal with plan/execute split to avoid double-counting when multiple corrections reference the same source transaction.
key_file_guides.watch_for: reissueBackfilledCredit re-issues purchased credits to FBO without triggering a new backfill sweep — intentional; do not replace with a recursive advance correction.

key_file_guides.role: collectCustomerFBO drains FBO sub-accounts in ascending creditPriority order into fboCollectionSource slices.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Ordering is ascending (lower priority drained first); changing cmp logic breaks priority-ordered credit consumption and breakage release alignment.

anti_patterns: Calling ledger.CommitGroup directly from charge handlers for FBO collection instead of routing through collector.Service
Modifying the correction plan execution order (correctionOrder preserves insertion order; reordering breaks per-transaction merging)
Assuming CorrectCollectedAccrued is idempotent — call it once per correction batch
Issuing advance shortfall for CreditThenInvoice mode (shouldAdvanceShortfall is CreditOnly-only)

decisions.decision: Correction uses plan-then-execute with merged per-transaction amounts.
decisions.rationale: Multiple realization corrections may reference the same original ledger transaction; merging before execution yields one aggregated correction per original transaction, preventing over-correction.

decisions.decision: Backfilled advance corrections re-issue to FBO rather than redirecting to another uncovered advance.
decisions.rationale: Prevents correction from triggering a cascading backfill pass that could unpredictably affect other outstanding advances.

code_examples.scenario: Calling collector.Service from a charge handler to collect FBO credits
code_examples.code:
realizations, err := h.collector.CollectToAccrued(ctx, collector.CollectToAccruedInput{
    Namespace:      input.Charge.Namespace,
    ChargeID:       input.Charge.ID,
    CustomerID:     input.Charge.Intent.CustomerID,
    Annotations:    chargeAnnotationsForFlatFeeCharge(input.Charge),
    At:             input.Charge.Intent.InvoiceAt,
    Currency:       input.Charge.Intent.Currency,
    SettlementMode: input.Charge.Intent.SettlementMode,
    ServicePeriod:  input.ServicePeriod,
    Amount:         input.PreTaxTotalAmount,
})
if err != nil { return nil, err }
if len(realizations) == 0 { return nil, nil }
return realizations, nil

openmeter/ledger/customerbalance.purpose: Computes customer credit balances by combining settled ledger account balances with pending charge impacts, exposing a Service interface and Facade for balance queries used by billing and the v3 credits API.

patterns.name: Bounded vs unbounded impact split in pending balance
patterns.description: chargePendingBalanceCalculator.CalculatePendingBalance separates CreditThenInvoice impact (bounded — capped at existing positive balance) from CreditOnly impact (unbounded — can drive balance negative as advance exposure).
patterns.example: pendingBalance := applyBoundedAmount(bookedBalance, boundedAmount); return pendingBalance.Sub(unboundedAmount)

patterns.name: Facade wrapping Service for validated access
patterns.description: Facade is a thin wrapper exposing GetBalances / GetBalance with validated inputs; NewFacade requires a non-nil Service. API handlers must use Facade, not Service directly.
patterns.example: func NewFacade(service Service) (*Facade, error) { if service == nil { return nil, errors.New("service is required") }; return &Facade{service: service}, nil }

patterns.name: Noop implementation for credits-disabled deployments
patterns.description: noop.go provides a no-op Service returning empty balances without touching the ledger; app/common wires it when credits.enabled=false. Must stay in sync with Service interface.
patterns.example: func (noopService) GetBalances(ctx context.Context, in GetBalancesInput) ([]BalanceByCurrency, error) { return nil, nil }

patterns.name: RealizedCredits type-switches on ChargeType
patterns.description: Impact.RealizedCredits() switches on meta.ChargeType (FlatFee vs UsageBased). CreditPurchase charges return zero realized credits because purchased credits live in FBO balance, not in realizations.
patterns.example: switch i.Type() { case meta.ChargeTypeFlatFee: charge, _ := i.AsFlatFeeCharge(); return charge.Realizations.CurrentRun.CreditRealizations.Sum() }

key_file_guides.role: Impact type and chargePendingBalanceCalculator; Impact derives RealizedCredits, OutstandingAmount, BoundedAmount, UnboundedAmount from a charge plus amount.
key_file_guides.watch_for: RealizedCredits switches on ChargeType — adding a new billing/charges charge type without a case here silently returns zero realized credits.

key_file_guides.role: GetBalancesInput, GetBalanceInput, BalanceByCurrency, and the Facade struct; entry point for v3 API handlers.
key_file_guides.watch_for: GetBalanceInput.After is an optional TransactionCursor for pagination; omitting it returns the full balance.

key_file_guides.role: Service interface and NewService, composing funded_loader, ledger_loader and chargePendingBalanceCalculator.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Service is the wiring boundary; app/common provides real impl or noop.go depending on credits.enabled.
Must be updated when the Service interface gains methods; the compile-time var _ assertion catches misses.

anti_patterns: Instantiating the real Service when credits.enabled=false (must use noop)
Calling Service methods directly without going through Facade when input validation is required
Adding pending-balance calculation logic outside calculation.go
Treating CreditPurchase charges as having realized credits in RealizedCredits() — they return zero

decisions.decision: Bounded vs unbounded impact split in CalculatePendingBalance.
decisions.rationale: CreditThenInvoice consumption is bounded by existing positive balance (cannot create credit debt); CreditOnly can drive balance negative as advance exposure. Mixing them into a single deduction would misrepresent available credit.

code_examples.scenario: Computing pending balance impact for a charge

openmeter/ledger/noop.purpose: Provides no-op implementations of all ledger interfaces (ledger.Ledger, ledger.AccountResolver, ledger.BalanceQuerier, ledgeraccount.Service, namespace.Handler) used when credits.enabled=false; every method returns zero values and nil errors without touching any database.

patterns.name: Compile-time interface assertions for every noop type
patterns.description: Each noop type has a var _ <interface> = <type>{} assertion so it stays in sync when interface methods are added. Removing them silently breaks the credits-disabled guarantee.
patterns.example:
var _ ledger.Ledger = Ledger{}
var _ ledger.AccountResolver = AccountResolver{}

patterns.name: Value-type structs for trivial construction
patterns.description: All noop types are value types (struct{} or minimal-field structs), letting Wire providers return them with zero-value syntax and no allocation.
patterns.example: func NewLedgerNamespaceHandler() noop.NamespaceHandler { return noop.NamespaceHandler{} }

patterns.name: Deterministic non-nil return values
patterns.description: Methods return minimal valid non-nil objects (e.g. CustomerAccounts with all three account types populated) so callers can dereference without nil checks.
patterns.example: return ledger.CustomerAccounts{FBOAccount: ..., ReceivableAccount: ..., AccruedAccount: ...}, nil

patterns.name: normalizeID / normalizeNamespace for empty-string safety
patterns.description: Helpers substitute a fallback string ('noop', 'noop-account') when namespace or ID is empty to prevent downstream panics when callers call .ID().
patterns.example: func normalizeID(id, fallback string) string { if id == "" { return fallback }; return id }

patterns.name: accountTypeForRoute for structurally valid sub-accounts
patterns.description: newSubAccount infers AccountType from Route fields (TransactionAuthorizationStatus -> Receivable, CreditPriority -> FBO, else Accrued) so sub-accounts pass interface type checks.
patterns.example: accountType := accountTypeForRoute(normalizedRoute)

key_file_guides.role: Single file containing all noop types, internal helpers, and every interface compliance assertion.
key_file_guides.watch_for: When any ledger interface gains methods, add matching stubs here immediately — the var _ assertions cause compile failure otherwise.

anti_patterns: Removing the var _ interface assertion lines (the only compile-time proof noop types stay compliant)
Adding any database calls or side-effects to noop methods (must remain pure no-ops)
Returning nil for interface-typed fields inside CustomerAccounts or BusinessAccounts (callers dereference without nil checks)
Using this package when credits.enabled=true (app/common must route to real implementations)

decisions.decision: All noop types are value types (struct{}) rather than pointers.
decisions.rationale: Simplifies Wire providers: return noop.Ledger{} and noop.AccountResolver{} without allocation or error handling.

code_examples.scenario: Wire provider returning noop implementations when credits are disabled

openmeter/ledger/recognizer.purpose: Implements revenue recognition by transferring attributable accrued ledger balances to earnings accounts using lineage-aware segment splitting, exposing a single RecognizeEarnings operation wrapped in a Postgres transaction.

patterns.name: Config struct with Validate() before NewService
patterns.description: Config holds all deps (Ledger, ResolverDependencies, Lineage, TransactionManager) and validates non-nil before NewService; NewService returns (Service, error).
patterns.example: func NewService(config Config) (Service, error) { if err := config.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err }; return &service{...}, nil }

patterns.name: transaction.Run wrapping recognition flow
patterns.description: RecognizeEarnings wraps all DB reads/writes inside transaction.Run for atomic segment splitting plus ledger commit.
patterns.example: return transaction.Run(ctx, s.transactionManager, func(ctx context.Context) (RecognizeEarningsResult, error) { ... })

patterns.name: Collect eligible lineages then resolve recognition template
patterns.description: Loads lineages via LoadLineagesByCustomer, filters to recognizable states (RealCredit, AdvanceBackfilled), sums totalEligible, then resolves RecognizeEarningsFromAttributableAccruedTemplate against the actual ledger balance.
patterns.example: eligible := collectEligibleLineages(lineages); resolved, _ := transactions.ResolveTransactions(ctx, s.deps, scope, RecognizeEarningsFromAttributableAccruedTemplate{Amount: totalEligible})

patterns.name: Segment splitting with remainder creation
patterns.description: For each eligible segment consumed=min(segRemaining, remaining); if consumed<segment amount a remainder segment is created, then an EarningsRecognized segment referencing the backing group for reversal traceability.
patterns.example: consumed := minDecimal(seg.Amount, remaining); if rem := seg.Amount.Sub(consumed); rem.IsPositive() { s.lnge.CreateSegment(ctx, lineage.CreateSegmentInput{Amount: rem, State: seg.State}) }

patterns.name: NoopService for tests not exercising recognition
patterns.description: NoopService implements Service (var _ Service = NoopService{}) and returns a zero RecognizeEarningsResult.
patterns.example: func (NoopService) RecognizeEarnings(context.Context, RecognizeEarningsInput) (RecognizeEarningsResult, error) { return RecognizeEarningsResult{}, nil }

key_file_guides.role: Service interface, Config, RecognizeEarningsInput (with Validate()), RecognizeEarningsResult, NewService.
key_file_guides.watch_for: RecognizeEarningsInput.Validate() requires CustomerID, non-zero At, and Currency — all three are mandatory.

key_file_guides.role: RecognizeEarnings impl: lineage loading, eligible segment collection, template resolution, segment splitting, ledger commit.
key_file_guides.watch_for: recognizableSegmentStates map (RealCredit, AdvanceBackfilled) governs eligibility — adding a new recognizable LineageSegmentState requires updating this map.

key_file_guides.role: NoopService returning zero results for tests and credits-disabled paths.
key_file_guides.watch_for: var _ Service = NoopService{} assertion ensures compile-time compliance as Service evolves.

anti_patterns: Calling RecognizeEarnings outside a Postgres transaction (transaction.Run is the required wrapper; must not be disabled)
Adding recognizable segment states without updating recognizableSegmentStates in recognize.go
Constructing recognizer.Service without calling Config.Validate() first
Skipping segment remainder creation when consumed < segment amount (leaves orphan balance in the lineage)

decisions.decision: Recognition resolves against the actual ledger accrued balance, not purely against lineage amounts.
decisions.rationale: The ledger balance may differ from summed lineage amounts due to manual adjustments or advance shortfall issuance; resolving against the ledger keeps the entry matched to real accounting state.

decisions.decision: Segment splitting stores SourceState and SourceBackingTransactionGroupID on EarningsRecognized segments.
decisions.rationale: Enables future correction to unwind recognition back to the prior state by restoring the original segment state and its backing group reference.
